content.text: In the village of Mamma, in Katsushika District of the province of Shimōsa, there lived a man named Katsushirō.

His family had long dwelt there since his grandfather’s time, owning many fields and living in prosperity. But when he came of age, his nature proved indifferent to practical affairs. He came to regard farming as hateful, and so, at last, the household fell into poverty.
